title: Affiliate link redirect & tracking
description: Introduce an internal outbound redirect for listing clicks that injects per-source affiliate/referral parameters server-side and records click events, without changing the visible listing experience or exposing affiliate config to the client. First of five independently shippable phases toward public/commercial readiness.
notes: See docs/adr/0001-phased-path-to-public-commercial-readiness.md and docs/adr/0002-affiliate-link-redirect-and-tracking.md. Shipped 2026-07-08 as a generalised 'Marketplace Outbound Gateway' (outbound.py) rather than an affiliate-only mechanism, per updated brief — see docs/implementation-notes/2026-07-08-2135-marketplace-outbound-gateway-epic-101.md and ARCHITECTURE.md section 9. 698 tests passing (665 prior + 33 new), zero regressions. No account/auth dependency — shipped standalone. Existing listing behaviour (destination, new-tab) unchanged in outcome; only the mechanism changed. Scope grew beyond the ADR's original 3 templates to also cover offers.html and the duplicate-review dup_side macro (same _MATCH_SELECT-backed rows, genuinely missed by the original ADR). Deferred: per-user attribution (needs EPIC-103), revenue reporting, multi-programme A/B testing, extending tracking to manual-assisted search links (Gumtree/Facebook/links-type sources have no listing_id), anonymous session identifiers (no session concept exists anywhere else in this app yet).
